brief_justification: Agent selects B but correct answer is C — the dismissal of C ('dismisses genuine evidential base') misreads C's actual claim about training loss vs. benchmark capability, making the answer wrong despite sound surrounding logic; Agent performs multi-level epistemic partitioning with KNOWN/KNOWABLE/UNKNOWABLE table and identifies second-order interface mismatch between training loss and benchmark performance; Agent names what it is amplifying and suppressing from the injection but does not explicitly flag potential biases in its own selection between B and C; Agent verifies by testing all four answer options against its framework and explaining eliminations, though does not sanity-check from a different angle; Structured uncertainty classification is explicit with per-claim zone labeling and confidence distinctions, meeting high epistemic honesty standards; All four options are evaluated with specific rejection reasons grounded in the epistemic framework; Every conclusion traces to the partitioning table and elimination steps, fully reproducible; Agent avoids propagating false confidence (the gate's core failure) but does not explicitly name the gate as a pattern it is blocking; All suppressed modes (false confidence, vague hedging, implicit compatibility assumption, interface mismatch tolerance) are absent and several are explicitly addressed; Response maps closely to the topology's scan→classify→rule-lookup→verify loop, with the table as ACC and the why-not sections as the G1 gate check.
